test.observation: Jest ran 44 suites / 392 tests and all passed. Output still included the expected polyfill diagnostic console.log noise, agent-store/agent-init console logging, and the wallet-connect error-path console.error from the existing test harness.

typecheck.observation: tsc --noEmit completed successfully with no TypeScript diagnostics.

lint.observation: ESLint exited 0 with no warnings or errors.

blockingIssues.description: `refreshIdentities()` now returns early when `agentDid` is absent, but the initialize/restore flows still call it immediately after `agent.initialize(...)` and nothing subscribes to `agentDid` becoming available. Because upstream only assigns `agentDid` during `agent.start()`, identity lists can remain stale instead of automatically retrying once the DID is observed.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented in `.factory/library/architecture.md` that upstream `@enbox/agent` leaves `agentDid` unset after `initialize()` and only assigns it during `start()`, so DID-backed refresh flows must wait for observation instead of assuming readiness immediately after initialize/restore.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten the handoff honesty gate so workers do not claim `followedProcedure: true` unless the transcript shows the required mission/context reads and, for non-trivial behavior changes, a red-first test cycle or an explicit documented deviation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `misc-suppress-agent-did-race-warnings` and `misc-polyfills-subtle-wrapper-test-env` reviews flagged workers marking `followedProcedure: true` despite missing required reads and/or no visible red-first test evidence in `worker-transcripts.jsonl`.
